title: Fears strikes in Lebanon and northern Israel are expanding beyond traditional strongholds | ABC NEWS
description: ABC News correspondent Bridget Rollason is in Israel, where Iran has joined forces with its proxy Hezbollah to carry out coordinated attacks.  Hundreds of rocket drones and missiles have been launched across Israel over the past few days.  More than 50 people were injured when an Iranian ballistic missile got through Israel's air defences on Friday and destroyed homes in the Arab Israeli village of Zarzir near Nazareth.  Nimran Grifat lives in the village and said he never expected Zarzir would be targeted.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. description: A fifth member of the Iranian women's football team has withdrawn her asylum claim and left the country. title: Denmark's royals visit the heart of Australia | ABC NEWS
description: King Frederik and Queen Mary have begun Denmark's first state visit down under in 40 years with a tour of the heart of Australia.  The royals were up before dawn today to walk the Kuniya track to the Muṯitjulu Waterhole, after taking in the sunset the day before.  Queen Mary told the ABC her first trip to Uluru was "magical".  The couple then headed to Canberra with a jam-packed itinerary, highlighted by a 21-gun salute at Government House and a dinner hosted by Governor-General Sam Mostyn. title: Fifth member of Iranian women's soccer team leaves Australia | ABC NEWS
description: A fifth member of the Iranian women's soccer team has withdrawn their claim of asylum and left the country overnight, the federal government has confirmed.  Seven team members, including a player handler, were granted humanitarian visas by the Australian government last week while they were here for the Asian Cup tournament.  Local Iranian diaspora members campaigning for the women to stay claim the staff member granted asylum was passing on threats from the Iranian regime.  Government sources say they've looked into those claims and have no reason to believe them.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: In 1979, severe petrol shortages in Australia led to fuel rationing | ABC NEWS
description: In mid-1979, Australia experienced severe petrol shortages that led to panic buying, driven by a drop in oil production after the Iranian Revolution and a national refinery operators' strike.  Governments responded with strict rationing, including a number plate system based on odd and even numbers for buying fuel. title: Ukraine, Hungary clash over crucial oil pipeline from Russia | The World | ABC News
description: The war in Ukraine is coming into sharp focus in Hungary, as the country goes to the polls next month. At the heart of a fresh dispute between the two countries is an oil pipeline that Ukraine says was damaged by Russian strikes in January. Oil has since stopped flowing to Hungary from the Druzhba pipeline that transports oil from Russia. And Hungary's Prime Minister Victor Orban is accusing Ukraine of deliberately delaying its reopening and has blocked both fresh EU sanctions on Moscow and a vital $US103bn loan for Kyiv until the pipeline is reopened. Lecturer in energy systems at Oxford, Dr Adi Imsirovic, spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the growing dispute between the two countries. title: How ship busted with 5 tonnes of cocaine ended up in Sydney | The World | ABC NEWS
description: A ship that was found to be carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine near French Polynesia earlier this year has been escorted into Sydney Harbour after issuing a distress call. French authorities intercepted the MV Raider in international waters near French Polynesia in January, and allowed the vessel and its 11 crew to continue sailing to Australia after dumping the drugs at sea. Pacific Beat reporter Lucy Cooper has been following the story and spoke with The World's Girish Sawlani about what will happen next to the MV Raider.
